title.en: Medical Device Regulatory and Surveillance Ontology

description.en: The medical device ontology is designed to structure and represent information about medical devices and post-market surveillance within the European regulatory framework (MDR/IVDR).

citation.value: Garcia Chavero, D. and Marin Chamorro, J. Medical Device Regulatory and Surveillance Ontology.

abstract.value: Ontology detailing medical devices, post-market surveillance, incidents, and field safety corrective actions compliant with EU MDR/IVDR regulations.

title.value: Medical Device Regulatory and Surveillance Ontology

publisher.value: FUOC - Fundació Universitat Oberta de Catalunya

bibliographicCitation.value: Garcia Chavero, D. and Marin Chamorro, J. Medical Device Regulatory and Surveillance Ontology. 2026.

comment.en: Risk of contamination leading to systematic diagnostic errors.
Status indicating that the action has been completed.
Risk associated with unintended ionizing radiation exposure.
A potential source of harm or adverse health effect associated with a device.
A device intended to be introduced into the human body.
Risk category involving direct injury to the patient.
Risk of physical harm to the patient.
Low likelihood of occurrence.
A medical instrument, apparatus, or machine used for healthcare purposes.
Incidents associated with critical risks, inferred by rules.
Risk of device-related infection or biofilm formation.
Risk associated with the physical materials of the device.
A device dependent on a source of energy other than the human body.
A device used in vitro for the examination of specimens.
The set of laws and regulations governing medical device safety.
High individual risk and high public health risk (IVDR).
Moderate to high individual risk and low to moderate public health risk (IVDR).
Functional classification of the medical device.
Risk related to exploitable software vulnerabilities enabling unauthorized access.
Risk category involving compromised safety of the device.
Devices that are both active and implantable, inferred by rules.
Corrective actions that have an official closure date.
Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
Highest risk class for medical devices.
Moderate level of severity.
Moderate to high risk class for medical devices.
Low to moderate risk class for medical devices.
High level of severity involving significant injury or impairment.
Highest level of severity representing extreme danger to life.
An active medical device intended to be partially or totally implanted.
High likelihood of occurrence.
The specific category of a Field Safety Corrective Action.
The specific nature of the danger represented by a risk.
An adverse event or malfunction associated with a medical device.
An incident that did not result in harm but had the potential to do so.
The current state of a corrective action or investigation process.
Classification of the nature and severity of an adverse event.
A broad grouping of risks based on their origin or impact.
Safety communication issued to users regarding a medical device.
Risk category involving incorrect diagnostic results.
An IVD device intended to be used by a lay person.
Manufacturer involved in incidents with critical severity.
Specific risk type representing potential diagnostic errors.
Status indicating that the action is still in progress.
Risk category involving inadequate performance of the device.
Urgent safety notice regarding the proper use of a device.
Risk category related to manufacturing or product defects.
The legal entity responsible for the design and compliance of the medical device.
Implantable device that requires an active energy source.
Risk category involving the breakdown or failure of the device.
Risk associated with software bugs or malfunctions.
Risk of unintended interference.
Medium likelihood of occurrence.
Corrective action performed directly on the device in the field.
The likelihood of a specific risk or hazard occurring.
A device intended to be used on one individual during a single procedure.
In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (EU) 2017/746.
Risk type indicating structural or functional failure.
Action taken to eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity or incident.
The measure of the possible consequences of a hazard.
An incident that directly or indirectly led to death or serious injury.
Classification of a device based on its risk to patients and users.
Action to return a device to the manufacturer.
The medical purpose for which the device is intended.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is intended for reuse.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is active.
The total number of device units affected by a corrective action.
Associates an incident with the specific risk it represents.
The official authorization or certification number of the manufacturer.
Specifies the specific nature of the danger represented by a risk.
Indicates the incident for which a specific corrective action was implemented.
Identifies the medical devices produced by a specific manufacturer.
A detailed textual narrative explaining the entity.
The descriptive name of the risk.
Boolean flag indicating whether the device is designed to be implanted.
Classifies a device, incident, or risk into a specific device functional type.
Associates a specific risk with the incident that materialized it.
The sex of the patient involved in the incident.
The result or consequence of the incident for the patient.
Indicates the risk classification of a medical device.
The age of the patient involved in the incident.
Identifies the manufacturer reporting the incident.
Associates a risk with its broad category.
Links an incident to the corrective action taken to address it.
The identifier for the Field Safety Corrective Action.
Links a corrective action to its specific typology.
The Single Registration Number for the manufacturer in EUDAMED.
The reference number for the Field Safety Corrective Action in EUDAMED.
The date when the corrective action was officially closed.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is an in vitro diagnostic device.
The date when the incident or risk was first detected.
The unique identifier assigned to a specific medical device unit or model.
Indicates the likelihood of a specific risk occurring.

The name of the manufacturer.

The reference number for the incident in EUDAMED.
The commercial or common name of the medical device.

Links a device to the regulations governing its use.
The national regulatory body overseeing the manufacturer.
